017package org.apache.juneau.commons.bean;
018
019import static java.util.Collections.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.BeanMeta.MethodType.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.function.Suppliers.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.AnnotationTraversal.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ReflectionUtils.*;
024import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
025import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
026import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
027import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.eq;
028import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
029import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
030
031import java.beans.*;
032import java.io.*;
033import java.lang.reflect.*;
034import java.util.*;
035import java.util.function.*;
036
037import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
038import org.apache.juneau.commons.function.*;
039import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
040import org.apache.juneau.commons.lang.*;
041import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
042import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.Visibility;
043import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
044
045/**
046 * Encapsulates all access to the properties of a bean class (like a souped-up {@link BeanInfo}).
047 *
048 * <h5 class='topic'>Description</h5>
049 *
050 * Uses introspection to find all the properties associated with this class.  If the
051 * {@link BeanType @BeanType} annotation is present on the class (or the marshalling-side
052 * {@code @Marshalled} sibling), that information is used to determine the properties on the class.
053 * Otherwise, the {@code BeanInfo} functionality in Java is used to determine the properties on the class.
054 *
055 * <h5 class='topic'>Bean property ordering</h5>
056 *
057 * The order of the properties are as follows:
058 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
059 *    <li>
060 *       If {@link BeanType#properties() @BeanType(properties)} is specified on the class, the order matches the
061 *       list of properties in the annotation.
062 *    <li>
063 *       Otherwise, the order is based on the following:
064 *       <ul>
065 *          <li>Public fields (same order as {@code Class.getFields()}).
066 *          <li>Properties returned by {@code BeanInfo.getPropertyDescriptors()}.
067 *          <li>Non-standard getters/setters with {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotation defined on them.
068 *       </ul>
069 * </ul>
070 *
071 * <h5 class='topic'>Thread safety</h5>
072 *
073 * Instances are effectively immutable after construction and are safe for concurrent read access.
074 * Construction is not thread-safe and should be completed before sharing an instance across threads.
075 *
076 *
077 * @param <T> The class type that this metadata applies to.
078 */
079@SuppressWarnings({
080   "java:S115", // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention (e.g., PROP_class)
081   "java:S1200" // Central bean-introspection type; high coupling to annotations/reflect/utils is inherent to its role
082})
083public class BeanMeta<T> {
084
085   // Property name constants
086   private static final String PROP_class = "class";
087   private static final String PROP_properties = "properties";
088
089   /**
090    * Represents the result of creating a BeanMeta, including the bean metadata and any reason why it's not a bean.
091    *
092    * @param <T> The bean type.
093    * @param beanMeta The bean metadata, or <jk>null</jk> if the class is not a bean.
094    * @param notABeanReason The reason why the class is not a bean, or <jk>null</jk> if it is a bean.
095    */
096   public record BeanMetaValue<T>(BeanMeta<T> beanMeta, String notABeanReason) {
097      public Optional<BeanMeta<T>> optBeanMeta() { return o(beanMeta()); }
098      Optional<String> optNotABeanReason() { return o(notABeanReason()); }
099   }
100
101   /**
102    * Possible property method types.
103    */
104   enum MethodType {
105      UNKNOWN, GETTER, SETTER, EXTRAKEYS;
106   }
107
108   /*
109    * Temporary getter/setter method struct used for calculating bean methods.
110    */
111   private static class BeanMethod {
112      private String propertyName;
113      private MethodType methodType;
114      private Method method;
115      private ClassInfo type;
116
117      private BeanMethod(String propertyName, MethodType type, Method method) {
118         this.propertyName = propertyName;
119         this.methodType = type;
120         this.method = method;
121         this.type = info(type == SETTER ? method.getParameterTypes()[0] : method.getReturnType());
122      }
123
124      @Override /* Overridden from Object */
125      public String toString() {
126         return method.toString();
127      }
128
129      /*
130       * Returns true if this method matches the class type of the specified property.
131       * Only meant to be used for setters.
132       */
133      private boolean matchesPropertyType(BeanPropertyMeta.Builder b) {
134         if (b == null)
135            return false;
136
137         // Don't do further validation if this is the "*" bean property.
138         if ("*".equals(b.name))
139            return true;
140
141         // Get the bean property type from the getter/field.
142         Class<?> pt = null;  // Uses raw Class<?> here because assignment checks are performed against Class.
143         if (nn(b.getter))
144            pt = b.getter.getReturnType().inner();
145         else if (nn(b.field))
146            pt = b.field.inner().getType();
147
148         // Matches if only a setter is defined.
149         if (pt == null)
150            return true;
151
152         // Doesn't match if not same type or super type as getter/field.
153         if (! type.isAssignableFrom(pt))
154            return false;
155
156         // If a setter was previously set, only use this setter if it's a closer
157         // match (e.g. prev type is a superclass of this type).
158         if (b.setter == null)
159            return true;
160
161         return type.isStrictChildOf(b.setter.getParameterTypes().get(0).inner());
162      }
163   }
164
165   /*
166    * Represents a bean constructor with its associated property names.
167    *
168    * @param constructor The constructor information.
169    * @param propertyNames The list of property names that correspond to the constructor parameters.
170    */
171   private record BeanConstructor(Optional<ConstructorInfo> constructor, List<String> args) {}
172
173   /**
174    * Creates a {@link BeanMeta} instance for the specified bean type info.
175    *
176    * <p>
177    * This is the marshalling-side factory entry point.  It attempts to create bean metadata for a class; if the class is
178    * determined to be a bean, the returned {@link BeanMetaValue} carries the {@link BeanMeta} instance and a
179    * <jk>null</jk> reason.  Otherwise it carries <jk>null</jk> for the bean metadata and a non-empty string explaining
180    * why the class is not a bean.
181    *
182    * <p>
183    * The bean filter is resolved internally from the {@code @Marshalled} / {@link BeanType @BeanType} annotations on the
184    * class via {@link BeanMetaInitializer#buildBeanFilter(BeanInfo)}.  See {@link #of(Class, BeanConfigContext)} for
185    * the pure bean-modeling-side entry point that does not require a {@link BeanInfo}.
186    *
187    * <h5 class='section'>Exception handling:</h5>
188    * <p>
189    * If a {@link RuntimeException} is thrown during bean metadata creation, it is caught and the exception message
190    * is returned as the {@code notABeanReason} with <jk>null</jk> for the bean metadata.
191    *
192    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
193    * <p class='bjava'>
194    *    <jc>// Create bean metadata for a class via the marshalling-side context.</jc>
195    *    BeanInfo&lt;Person&gt; <jv>cm</jv> = <jv>marshallingContext</jv>.getClassMeta(Person.<jk>class</jk>);
196    *    BeanMetaHolder&lt;Person&gt; <jv>result</jv> = BeanMeta.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>cm</jv>, <jk>null</jk>);
197    *
198    *    <jc>// Check if it's a bean</jc>
199    *    <jk>if</jk> (<jv>result</jv>.beanMeta() != <jk>null</jk>) {
200    *       BeanMeta&lt;Person&gt; <jv>bm</jv> = <jv>result</jv>.beanMeta();
201    *       <jc>// Use the bean metadata...</jc>
202    *    } <jk>else</jk> {
203    *       String <jv>reason</jv> = <jv>result</jv>.notABeanReason();
204    *       <jc>// Handle the case where it's not a bean...</jc>
205    *    }
206    * </p>
207    *
208    * @param <T> The class type.
209    * @param cm The bean type info for the class to create bean metadata for.
210    * @param implClass Optional implementation class info to use when looking for a no-arg constructor.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
211    * @return A {@link BeanMetaValue} containing the bean metadata (if successful) or a reason why it's not a bean.
212    */
213   public static <T> BeanMetaValue<T> create(BeanInfo<T> cm, ClassInfo implClass) {
214      try {
215         var cfg = cm.getBeanConfigContext();
216         var ap = cfg.getAnnotationProvider();
217         var bmi = cfg.getBeanMetaInitializer();
218
219         // Sanity checks first.
220         if (cfg.isNotABean(cm))
221            return notABean("Class matches exclude-class list");
222
223         if (cfg.isBeansRequireSerializable() && ! cm.isAssignableTo(Serializable.class) && ! bmi.hasBeanRegistrationAnnotation(cfg, cm))
224            return notABean("Class is not serializable");
225
226         if (ap.has(BeanIgnore.class, cm))
227            return notABean("Class is annotated with @BeanIgnore");
228
229      if ((! cfg.getBeanClassVisibility().isVisible(cm.getModifiers()) || cm.isAnonymousClass()) && ! bmi.hasBeanRegistrationAnnotation(cfg, cm))
230         return notABean("Class is not public");
231
232         var bm = new BeanMeta<>(cm, bmi.buildBeanFilter(cm), null, implClass);
233
234         if (nn(bm.notABeanReason))
235            return notABean(bm.notABeanReason);
236
237         return new BeanMetaValue<>(bm, null);
238      } catch (RuntimeException e) {
239         return new BeanMetaValue<>(null, e.getMessage());
240      }
241   }
242
243   /*
244    * Extracts the property name from {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} or {@link Name @Name} annotations.
245    *
246    * <p>
247    * If {@link Name @Name} annotations are present, returns the value from the last one.
248    * Otherwise, searches through {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotations and returns the first non-empty
249    * {@link BeanProp#value() value()} or {@link BeanProp#name() name()} found.
250    *
251    * @param p List of {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotations.
252    * @param n List of {@link Name @Name} annotations.
253    * @return The property name, or <jk>null</jk> if no name is found.
254    */
255   private static String bpName(List<BeanProp> p, List<Name> n) {
256      if (p.isEmpty() && n.isEmpty())
257         return null;
258      if (! n.isEmpty())
259         return last(n).value();
260
261      var name = Holder.of(p.isEmpty() ? null : "");
262      p.forEach(x -> {
263         if (! x.value().isEmpty())
264            name.set(x.value());
265         if (! x.name().isEmpty())
266            name.set(x.name());
267      });
268
269      return name.orElse(null);
270   }
271
272   /*
273    * Extracts the property name from a single {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} or {@link Name @Name} annotation.
274    *
275    * <p>
276    * For {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotations, returns the first non-empty value found in the following order:
277    * <ol>
278    *    <li>{@link BeanProp#name() name()}
279    *    <li>{@link BeanProp#value() value()}
280    * </ol>
281    *
282    * <p>
283    * For {@link Name @Name} annotations, returns the {@link Name#value() value()}.
284    *
285    * <p>
286    * This method is used to extract property names from individual annotations, typically when processing
287    * annotation lists in stream operations.
288    *
289    * @param ai The annotation info containing either a {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} or {@link Name @Name} annotation.
290    * @return The property name extracted from the annotation, or <jk>null</jk> if no name is found.
291    * @see #bpName(List, List)
292    */
293   private static String name(AnnotationInfo<?> ai) {
294      if (ai.isType(BeanProp.class)) {
295         var p = ai.cast(BeanProp.class).inner();
296         if (ine(p.name()))
297            return p.name();
298         if (ine(p.value()))
299            return p.value();
300      } else {
301         var n = ai.cast(Name.class).inner();
302         if (ine(n.value()))
303            return n.value();
304      }
305      return null;
306   }
307
308   /*
309    * Shortcut for creating a BeanMetaValue with a not-a-bean reason.
310    */
311   private static <T> BeanMetaValue<T> notABean(String reason) {
312      return new BeanMetaValue<>(null, reason);
313   }
314
315   private BeanConstructor beanConstructor;                                   // The constructor for this bean.
316   private final Object marshallingContext;                                   // MarshallingContext, but Object-typed so the field can live in commons.bean.  Cast to MarshallingContext at marshalling-side use sites.  Null when constructed via {@link #of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}.
317   private final BeanConfigContext config;                                    // Bean-modeling settings facade — always non-null.  Sources: marshallingContext.getBeanConfigContext() (marshalling-side) or the explicit BeanConfigContext (commons-side).
318   private final BeanFilter beanFilter;                                       // Optional bean filter associated with the target class.  Typed as the bean-modeling-side SPI seam; marshalling-side callers narrow back to MarshalledFilter (the only concrete in-tree implementation).
319   private final NullableSupplier<InvocationHandler> beanProxyInvocationHandler;  // The invocation handler for this bean (if it's an interface).
320   private final Supplier<BeanRegistryLookup> beanRegistry;                   // The bean registry for this bean.  Exposed through the BeanRegistryLookup SPI seam.
321   private final Supplier<List<ClassInfo>> classHierarchy;                    // List of all classes traversed in the class hierarchy.
322   private final BeanInfo<T> classMeta;                                       // The target class type that this meta object describes.  Null when constructed via {@link #of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}.  Typed against the bean-modeling SPI seam.
323   private final ClassInfo classInfo;                                         // Pure-reflection view of the bean class that decouples bean modeling from ClassMeta.  Always non-null.
324   private final Supplier<String> dictionaryName;                             // The @Marshalled(typeName) annotation defined on this bean class.
325   private final BeanPropertyMeta dynaProperty;                               // "extras" property.
326   @SuppressWarnings({
327      "rawtypes" // Raw type required at this call site; generic type is verified at runtime
328   })
329   private final Class<? extends BeanFactory> factoryClass;  // @BeanType(factory=X.class) — null means no factory.
330   private final boolean fluentSetters;                                       // Whether fluent setters are enabled.
331   private final Map<Method,String> getterProps;                              // The getter properties on the target class.
332   private final Map<String,BeanPropertyMeta> hiddenProperties;               // The hidden properties on the target class.
333   private final ConstructorInfo implClassConstructor;                        // Optional constructor to use if one cannot be found.
334   private final String notABeanReason;                                       // Readable string explaining why this class wasn't a bean.
335   private final Map<String,BeanPropertyMeta> properties;                     // The properties on the target class.
336   private final Map<Method,String> setterProps;                              // The setter properties on the target class.
337   private final boolean unsortedProperties;                                  // Whether properties should use natural JVM-dependent order.
338   private final ClassInfo stopClass;                                         // The stop class for hierarchy traversal.
339   private final BeanPropertyMeta typeProperty;                               // "_type" mock bean property.
340   private final String typePropertyName;                                     // "_type" property actual name.
341   private final Map<BeanPropertyMeta,BeanRegistryLookup> propertyBeanRegistries;  // Per-property bean-registry side-map that keeps registry implementation details off BeanPropertyMeta itself. Typed against the commons.bean SPI seam.
342
343   /**
344    * Creates a {@link BeanMeta} for the specified class using the supplied {@link BeanConfigContext}.
345    *
346    * <p>
347    * This is the bean-modeling entry point — it constructs a {@link BeanMeta} purely from a {@link Class}
348    * and a {@link BeanConfigContext}, without touching any marshalling-side type infrastructure.
349    * The returned {@link BeanMeta} carries enough information to do raw getter/setter invocation and property
350    * iteration; marshalling-aware reads ({@link #getBeanInfo()}, type-resolution on each
351    * {@link BeanPropertyMeta#getBeanInfo()}, the per-property registry lookup) remain <jk>null</jk>.
352    *
353    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
354    * <p class='bjava'>
355    *    BeanMeta&lt;Person&gt; <jv>bm</jv> = BeanMeta.<jsm>of</jsm>(Person.<jk>class</jk>, BeanConfigContext.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>);
356    *    BeanPropertyMeta <jv>name</jv> = <jv>bm</jv>.getProperties().get(<js>"name"</js>);
357    * </p>
358    *
359    * @param <T> The bean class type.
360    * @param beanClass The class to build a {@link BeanMeta} for.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
361    * @param config The bean-modeling configuration to apply.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
362    * @return A new {@link BeanMeta} for the specified class.
363    */
364   public static <T> BeanMeta<T> of(Class<T> beanClass, BeanConfigContext config) {
365      return new BeanMeta<>(beanClass, config);
366   }
367
368   /**
369    * Same as {@link #of(Class, BeanConfigContext)} using {@link BeanConfigContext#DEFAULT}.
370    *
371    * @param <T> The bean class type.
372    * @param beanClass The class to build a {@link BeanMeta} for.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
373    * @return A new {@link BeanMeta} for the specified class.
374    */
375   public static <T> BeanMeta<T> of(Class<T> beanClass) {
376      return of(beanClass, BeanConfigContext.DEFAULT);
377   }
378
379   /**
380    * Constructor.
381    *
382    * <p>
383    * Creates a new {@link BeanMeta} instance for the specified class metadata. This constructor performs
384    * introspection to discover all bean properties, methods, and fields in the class hierarchy.
385    *
386    * <p>
387    * The bean metadata is built by:
388    * <ul>
389    *    <li>Finding all bean fields in the class hierarchy
390    *    <li>Finding all bean methods (getters, setters, extraKeys) in the class hierarchy
391    *    <li>Determining the appropriate constructor for bean instantiation
392    *    <li>Building the class hierarchy for property discovery
393    *    <li>Creating the bean registry for dictionary name resolution
394    *    <li>Validating and filtering properties based on bean filter settings
395    * </ul>
396    *
397    * @param cm The class metadata for the bean class.
398    * @param bf Optional bean filter to apply. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
399    * @param pNames Explicit list of property names and order. If <jk>null</jk>, properties are determined automatically.
400    * @param implClass Optional implementation class constructor to use if one cannot be found. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
401    */
402   protected BeanMeta(BeanInfo<T> cm, BeanFilter bf, String[] pNames, ClassInfo implClass) {
403      this(cm, cm, cm.getBeanConfigContext(), cm.getMarshallingContext(), bf, pNames, implClass);
404   }
405
406   /**
407    * Bean-modeling constructor — builds a {@link BeanMeta} without marshalling context state.
408    *
409    * <p>
410    * See {@link #of(Class, BeanConfigContext)} for the public entry point.  The {@link #getBeanInfo()},
411    * {@link #getBeanTypeResolver() bean type resolver}, and per-property registry lookups are
412    * left <jk>null</jk>; the per-property {@link BeanPropertyMeta#getBeanInfo() rawTypeMeta}/{@code typeMeta}
413    * fields are also <jk>null</jk> (no type-resolution is performed in this path).
414    *
415    * @param beanClass The bean class.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
416    * @param config The bean-modeling configuration.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
417    */
418   protected BeanMeta(Class<T> beanClass, BeanConfigContext config) {
419      this(null, info(assertArgNotNull("beanClass", beanClass)), assertArgNotNull("config", config), null, null, null, null);
420   }
421
422   @SuppressWarnings({
423      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for bean metadata initialization
424      "java:S107"   // 7 parameters needed to support both construction paths
425   })
426   private BeanMeta(BeanInfo<T> cm, ClassInfo ci0, BeanConfigContext config, Object mc, BeanFilter bf, String[] pNames, ClassInfo implClass) {
427      classMeta = cm;
428      classInfo = ci0;
429      this.config = config;
430      marshallingContext = mc;
431      beanFilter = bf;
432      implClassConstructor = o(implClass).map(x -> x.getPublicConstructor(x2 -> x2.hasNumParameters(0)).orElse(null)).orElse(null);
433      fluentSetters = config.isFindFluentSetters() || (nn(bf) && bf.isFluentSetters());
434      stopClass = o(bf).map(x -> x.getStopClass()).orElse(info(Object.class));
435      beanRegistry = memoize(this::findBeanRegistry);
436      classHierarchy = memoize(this::findClassHierarchy);
437      beanConstructor = findBeanConstructor();
438
439      // Local variables for initialization
440      var ap = config.getAnnotationProvider();
441      var c = classInfo.inner();
442      var ci = classInfo;
443      String notABeanReasonTemp = null;
444      var propertiesValue = Holder.<Map<String,BeanPropertyMeta>>empty();
445      var hiddenPropertiesMap = CollectionUtils.<String,BeanPropertyMeta>map();
446      var getterPropsMap = CollectionUtils.<Method,String>map();  // Convert to MethodInfo keys
447      var setterPropsMap = CollectionUtils.<Method,String>map();
448      var dynaPropertyValue = Holder.<BeanPropertyMeta>empty();
449      var propertyBeanRegistriesTemp = CollectionUtils.<BeanPropertyMeta,BeanRegistryLookup>map();  // Per-property BeanRegistry side-map.
450      var unsortedPropertiesTemp = false;
451      var btList = ap.find(BeanType.class, classInfo);
452      var propertyNamer = o(bf).map(x -> x.getPropertyNamer()).orElse(config.getPropertyNamer());
453
454      // resolveTypePropertyName may return null on the commons-side NOOP path; fall back to the configured default.
455      var resolvedTypePropertyName = config.getBeanMetaInitializer().resolveTypePropertyName(config, classInfo);
456      this.typePropertyName = nn(resolvedTypePropertyName) ? resolvedTypePropertyName : config.getBeanTypePropertyName();
457
458      // Check if constructor is required but not found (records are exempt since they use canonical constructors)
459      if (! beanConstructor.constructor().isPresent() && bf == null && config.isBeansRequireDefaultConstructor() && ! ci.isRecord())
460         notABeanReasonTemp = "Class does not have the required no-arg constructor";
461
462      var bfo = o(bf);
463      var fixedBeanProps = bfo.map(x -> x.getProperties()).orElse(emptySet());
464
465      try {
466         Map<String,BeanPropertyMeta.Builder> normalProps = map();  // NOAI
467
468         // First populate the properties with those specified in the bean annotation to
469         // ensure that ordering first.
470         fixedBeanProps.forEach(x -> normalProps.put(x, BeanPropertyMeta.builder(this, x)));
471
472         if (config.isUseJavaBeanIntrospector()) {
473            var c2 = bfo.map(x -> x.getInterfaceClass()).filter(Objects::nonNull).orElse(classInfo);
474            java.beans.BeanInfo bi = null;
475            if (! c2.isInterface())
476               bi = Introspector.getBeanInfo(c2.inner(), stopClass.inner());
477            else
478               bi = Introspector.getBeanInfo(c2.inner(), null);
479            if (nn(bi))
480               mergeJavaBeanPropertyDescriptorsIntoNormalProps(bi, normalProps, propertyNamer);
481
482         } else /* Use 'better' introspection */ {
483
484            findBeanFields().forEach(x -> {
485               var name = ap.find(x).stream()
486                  .filter(x2 -> x2.isType(BeanProp.class) || x2.isType(Name.class))
487                  .map(BeanMeta::name)
488                  .filter(Objects::nonNull)
489                  .findFirst()
490                  .orElse(propertyNamer.getPropertyName(x.getName()));
491               name = resolveBeanFieldPropertyName(x, name, propertyNamer);
492               if (nn(name)) {
493                  normalProps.computeIfAbsent(name, n->BeanPropertyMeta.builder(this, n)).setField(x);
494               }
495            });
496
497            var bms = findBeanMethods();
498
499            // Iterate through all the getters.
500            bms.forEach(x -> {
501               var pn = x.propertyName;
502               var m = x.method;  // Method is read first for name/annotation precedence checks, then wrapped as MethodInfo.
503               var mi = info(m);
504               var bpm = normalProps.computeIfAbsent(pn, k -> new BeanPropertyMeta.Builder(this, k));
505
506               if (x.methodType == GETTER) {
507                  // Two getters.  Pick the best.
508                  if (nn(bpm.getter)
509                     && ((! ap.has(BeanProp.class, mi) && ap.has(BeanProp.class, bpm.getter))
510                        || (m.getName().startsWith("is") && bpm.getter.getNameSimple().startsWith("get")))) {
511                     // @BeanProp on existing getter takes precedence; else getX() overrides isX().
512                     m = bpm.getter.inner();
513                  }
514                  bpm.setGetter(info(m));
515               }
516            });
517
518            // Now iterate through all the setters.
519            bms.stream().filter(x -> eq(x.methodType, SETTER)).forEach(x -> {
520               var bpm = normalProps.get(x.propertyName);
521               if (x.matchesPropertyType(bpm))
522                  bpm.setSetter(info(x.method));
523            });
524
525            // Now iterate through all the extraKeys.
526            bms.stream().filter(x -> eq(x.methodType, EXTRAKEYS)).forEach(x -> normalProps.get(x.propertyName).setExtraKeys(info(x.method)));
527         }
528
529         var typeVarImpls = TypeVariables.of(c);
530
531         // Eliminate invalid properties, and set the contents of getterProps and setterProps.
532         var readOnlyProps = bfo.map(x -> x.getReadOnlyProperties()).orElse(emptySet());
533         var writeOnlyProps = bfo.map(x -> x.getWriteOnlyProperties()).orElse(emptySet());
534         for (var i = normalProps.values().iterator(); i.hasNext();) {
535            var p = i.next();
536            validateAndRegisterProperty(p, c, typeVarImpls, readOnlyProps, writeOnlyProps, i, getterPropsMap, setterPropsMap);
537         }
538
539         // Check for missing properties.
540         fixedBeanProps.stream().filter(x -> ! normalProps.containsKey(x)).findFirst().ifPresent(x -> { throw brex(c, "The property ''{0}'' was defined on the @BeanType(properties=X) annotation of class ''{1}'' but was not found on the class definition.", x, ci.getNameSimple()); });
541
542         // For records with renamed properties, remap constructor args to use the actual property names.
543         if (ci.isRecord() && ine(beanConstructor.args())) {
544            var components = ci.getRecordComponents();
545            var remappedArgs = new ArrayList<String>(beanConstructor.args().size());
546            for (int idx = 0; idx < beanConstructor.args().size(); idx++) {
547               var componentName = beanConstructor.args().get(idx);
548               if (normalProps.containsKey(componentName)) {
549                  remappedArgs.add(componentName);
550               } else {
551                  var rcName = idx < components.size() ? components.get(idx).getName() : componentName;
552                  var found = normalProps.entrySet().stream()
553                     .filter(e -> (e.getValue().field != null && e.getValue().field.hasName(rcName))
554                        || (e.getValue().getter != null && e.getValue().getter.hasName(rcName)))
555                     .map(Map.Entry::getKey)
556                     .findFirst()
557                     .orElse(componentName);
558                  remappedArgs.add(found);
559               }
560            }
561            beanConstructor = new BeanConstructor(beanConstructor.constructor(), remappedArgs);
562         }
563
564         // Mark constructor arg properties.
565         for (var fp : beanConstructor.args()) {
566            var m = normalProps.get(fp);
567            if (m == null)
568               throw brex(c, "The property ''{0}'' was defined on the @BeanCtor(properties=X) annotation but was not found on the class definition.", fp);
569            m.setAsConstructorArg();
570         }
571
572         // Make sure at least one property was found (records with no components are exempt).
573         if (bf == null && config.isBeansRequireSomeProperties() && normalProps.isEmpty() && ! ci.isRecord())
574            notABeanReasonTemp = "No properties detected on bean class";
575
576         unsortedPropertiesTemp = config.isUnsortedProperties() || bfo.map(x -> x.isUnsortedProperties()).orElse(false) || !fixedBeanProps.isEmpty();
577
578         propertiesValue.set(unsortedPropertiesTemp ? map() : sortedMap());
579
580      normalProps.forEach((k, v) -> {
581         var pMeta = v.build();
582         if (pMeta.isDyna())
583            dynaPropertyValue.set(pMeta);
584         propertiesValue.get().put(k, pMeta);
585         // Build the property-level BeanRegistry side-map entry from the builder's accumulated
586         // dictionary classes.  Parents to the bean-level registry so @MarshalledProp(dictionary={})
587         // entries chain on top of bean and global dictionaries.  Skipped on the commons-side path
588         // (no marshallingContext means no BeanRegistry construction).
589         if (nn(marshallingContext) && nn(v.dictionaryClasses))
590            propertyBeanRegistriesTemp.put(pMeta, config.getBeanMetaInitializer().buildPropertyBeanRegistry(marshallingContext, beanRegistry.get(), v.dictionaryClasses));
591      });
592
593         // If a beanFilter is defined, look for inclusion and exclusion lists.
594         if (bf != null) {
595
596            // Eliminated excluded properties if MarshalledFilter.excludeKeys is specified.
597            var bfbpi = bf.getProperties();
598            var bfbpx = bf.getExcludeProperties();
599            var p = propertiesValue.get();
600
601            if (! bfbpi.isEmpty()) {
602               // Only include specified properties if MarshalledFilter.includeKeys is specified.
603               // Note that the order must match includeKeys.
604               Map<String,BeanPropertyMeta> p2 = map();  // NOAI
605               bfbpi.stream().filter(p::containsKey).forEach(x -> p2.put(x, p.remove(x)));
606               hiddenPropertiesMap.putAll(p);
607               propertiesValue.set(p2);
608            }
609
610            bfbpx.forEach(x -> hiddenPropertiesMap.put(x, propertiesValue.get().remove(x)));
611         }
612
613         if (nn(pNames)) {
614            var p = propertiesValue.get();
615            Map<String,BeanPropertyMeta> p2 = map();
616            for (var k : pNames) {
617               if (p.containsKey(k))
618                  p2.put(k, p.get(k));
619               else
620                  hiddenPropertiesMap.put(k, p.get(k));
621            }
622            propertiesValue.set(p2);
623         }
624
625      } catch (BeanRuntimeException e) {
626         throw e;
627      } catch (Exception e) {
628         notABeanReasonTemp = "Exception:  " + getStackTrace(e);
629      }
630
631      notABeanReason = notABeanReasonTemp;
632      properties = u(propertiesValue.get());
633      hiddenProperties = u(hiddenPropertiesMap);
634      getterProps = u(getterPropsMap);
635      setterProps = u(setterPropsMap);
636      dynaProperty = dynaPropertyValue.get();
637      unsortedProperties = unsortedPropertiesTemp;
638      typeProperty = BeanPropertyMeta.builder(this, typePropertyName).canRead().canWrite().rawMetaType(String.class).build();
639      // Map the synthetic "_type" property to the bean-level BeanRegistry so consumers calling
640      // typeProperty.getBeanRegistry() (currently none in-tree, but a public API path) get the same
641      // registry the property previously carried as a field.  Skipped on the commons-side path.
642      if (nn(marshallingContext))
643         propertyBeanRegistriesTemp.put(typeProperty, beanRegistry.get());
644      propertyBeanRegistries = u(propertyBeanRegistriesTemp);
645      dictionaryName = memoize(this::findDictionaryName);
646      beanProxyInvocationHandler = memoize(() -> config.isUseInterfaceProxies() && classInfo.isInterface() ? new BeanProxyInvocationHandler<>(this) : null);
647      var factoryClassTemp = btList.stream().map(x -> x.inner().factory()).filter(x -> x != BeanFactory.Void.class).findFirst().orElse(null);
648      factoryClass = factoryClassTemp;
649   }
650
651   @SuppressWarnings({
652      "java:S107" // 8 parameters needed for property validation context
653   })
654   private void validateAndRegisterProperty(BeanPropertyMeta.Builder p, Class<?> c, TypeVariables typeVarImpls, Set<String> readOnlyProps, Set<String> writeOnlyProps, Iterator<BeanPropertyMeta.Builder> i, Map<Method,String> getterProps, Map<Method,String> setterProps) {
655      try {
656         if (p.field == null)
657            findInnerBeanField(p.name).ifPresent(p::setInnerField);
658
659         // When constructed via the commons-side path, marshallingContext is null and validate() runs in
660         // raw-reflection mode (no ClassMeta/ObjectSwap/BeanRegistry resolution).
661         if (p.validate((BeanTypeResolver) marshallingContext, typeVarImpls, readOnlyProps, writeOnlyProps)) {
662
663            // Marshalling-side post-processor — applies @MarshalledProp / @Swap annotation effects
664            // (swap detection, properties override, dictionary classes) and installs swap-aware
665            // read/write transforms.  Skipped on the commons-side path: those annotations require a
666            // MarshallingContext to resolve ObjectSwap/StringFormatSwap/Surrogate types and the swap
667            // class meta.
668            if (nn(marshallingContext))
669               config.getBeanPropertyPostProcessor().process(marshallingContext, p);
670
671            if (nn(p.getter))
672               getterProps.put(p.getter.inner(), p.name);
673
674            if (nn(p.setter))
675               setterProps.put(p.setter.inner(), p.name);
676
677         } else {
678            i.remove();
679         }
680      } catch (Exception e) {
681         throw brex(c, localizedMessage(e));
682      }
683   }
684
685   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
686   public boolean equals(Object o) {
687      return (o instanceof BeanMeta<?> o2) && eq(this, o2, (x, y) -> eq(x.classInfo, y.classInfo));
688   }
689
690   /**
691    * Returns the bean filter associated with this bean.
692    *
693    * <p>
694    * Bean filters are used to control aspects of how beans are handled during serialization and parsing, such as
695    * property inclusion/exclusion, property ordering, and type name mapping.
696    *
697    * <p>
698    * On the marshalling-side construction path, the bean filter is typically built from the {@code @Marshalled} and
699    * {@link BeanType @BeanType} annotations on the class.  If neither annotation is present (or this {@link BeanMeta}
700    * was built via the commons-side path), this method returns <jk>null</jk>.
701    *
702    * @return The bean filter for this bean, or <jk>null</jk> if no bean filter is associated with this bean.
703    */
704   public BeanFilter getBeanFilter() {
705      // The field is typed as the bean-modeling-side BeanFilter SPI; marshalling-side callers
706      // narrow back to MarshalledFilter (the only concrete implementation in-tree).
707      return beanFilter;
708   }
709
710   /**
711    * Returns the proxy invocation handler for this bean if it's an interface.
712    *
713    * @return The invocation handler, or <jk>null</jk> if this is not an interface or interface proxies are disabled.
714    */
715   public InvocationHandler getBeanProxyInvocationHandler() {
716      return beanProxyInvocationHandler.get();
717   }
718
719   /**
720    * Returns the bean registry for this bean.
721    *
722    * <p>
723    * The bean registry is used to resolve dictionary names to class types. It's created when a bean class has a
724    * {@code @Marshalled(dictionary)} annotation that specifies a list of possible subclasses.
725    *
726    * <p>
727    * Returns the bean-modeling-side SPI type ({@link BeanRegistryLookup}).
728    *
729    * @return The bean registry for this bean, or <jk>null</jk> if no bean registry is associated with it.
730    */
731   public BeanRegistryLookup getBeanRegistry() { return beanRegistry.get(); }
732
733   /**
734    * Returns the per-property registry lookup associated with the given {@link BeanPropertyMeta}.
735    *
736    * <p>
737    * {@link BeanPropertyMeta} no longer carries a concrete registry field — the per-property
738    * registry now lives in a side-map on this {@link BeanMeta} keyed by the property meta itself.  The serializer
739    * and parser sides still need to look up the property-level registry for polymorphic dispatch; they now route through
740    * this accessor (directly or via the deprecated-style {@link BeanPropertyMeta#getBeanRegistry()} delegate).
741    *
742    * <p>
743    * The returned registry chains the property's
744    * {@code @MarshalledProp(dictionary)} entries on top of
745    * the bean-level registry, which in turn chains on top of the global bean dictionary.
746    *
747    * @param p The bean property meta to look up.  Can be a normal property, the synthetic <js>"_type"</js> property,
748    *    or any other property meta produced by this bean.
749    * @return The bean registry for the specified property, or <jk>null</jk> if none was registered (e.g. the property
750    *    belongs to a different bean meta).
751    */
752   public BeanRegistryLookup getPropertyBeanRegistry(BeanPropertyMeta p) {
753      return propertyBeanRegistries.get(p);
754   }
755
756   /**
757    * Returns the {@link BeanInfo} of this bean.
758    *
759    * <p>
760    * Returns <jk>null</jk> when this {@link BeanMeta} was constructed via the commons-side path
761    * ({@link #of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}) — in that case {@link #getClassInfo()} carries the
762    * pure-reflection view of the bean class.
763    *
764    * <p>
765    * Returns the bean-modeling-side SPI type ({@link BeanInfo}).
766    *
767    * @return The {@link BeanInfo} of this bean, or <jk>null</jk> for bean-modeling-only construction.
768    */
769   public BeanInfo<T> getBeanInfo() { return classMeta; }
770
771   /**
772    * Returns the {@link ClassInfo} of this bean.
773    *
774    * <p>
775    * Pure-reflection view of the bean class.  Always non-null, regardless of construction path.  Use this in
776    * preference to {@link #getBeanInfo()} for any bean-modeling read that does not depend on marshalling-aware
777    * type metadata; the commons-side construction path leaves {@link #getBeanInfo()} <jk>null</jk>.
778    *
779    * @return The class info for this bean.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
780    */
781   public ClassInfo getClassInfo() { return classInfo; }
782
783   /**
784    * Returns the bean-modeling configuration snapshot used to build this {@link BeanMeta}.
785    *
786    * <p>
787    * For marshalling-side construction, this is sourced from the owning marshalling context.
788    * For commons-side construction (via {@link #of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}), this is the {@link BeanConfigContext}
789    * passed to the factory.  Always non-null.
790    *
791    * @return The bean-modeling configuration.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
792    */
793   public BeanConfigContext getConfig() { return config; }
794
795   /**
796    * Returns the dictionary name for this bean as defined through the {@code @Marshalled(typeName)} annotation.
797    *
798    * @return The dictionary name for this bean, or <jk>null</jk> if it has no dictionary name defined.
799    */
800   public String getDictionaryName() { return dictionaryName.get(); }
801
802   /**
803    * Returns a map of all properties on this bean.
804    *
805    * <p>
806    * The map is keyed by property name and contains {@link BeanPropertyMeta} objects that provide metadata about each
807    * property, including its type, getter/setter methods, field information, and serialization/parsing behavior.
808    *
809    * <p>
810    * This map contains only the normal (non-hidden) properties of the bean. Hidden properties can be accessed via
811    * {@link #getHiddenProperties()}.
812    *
813    * @return A map of property names to their metadata. The map is unmodifiable.
814    * @see #getPropertyMeta(String)
815    * @see #getHiddenProperties()
816    */
817   public Map<String,BeanPropertyMeta> getProperties() { return properties; }
818
819   /**
820    * Returns metadata about the specified property.
821    *
822    * @param name The name of the property on this bean.
823    * @return The metadata about the property, or <jk>null</jk> if no such property exists on this bean.
824    */
825   public BeanPropertyMeta getPropertyMeta(String name) {
826      if (name == null)
827         return dynaProperty;
828      var bpm = properties.get(name);
829      if (bpm == null)
830         bpm = hiddenProperties.get(name);
831      if (bpm == null)
832         bpm = dynaProperty;
833      return bpm;
834   }
835
836   /**
837    * Returns a mock bean property that resolves to the name <js>"_type"</js> and whose value always resolves to the
838    * dictionary name of the bean.
839    *
840    * @return The type name property.
841    */
842   public BeanPropertyMeta getTypeProperty() { return typeProperty; }
843
844   /**
845    * Returns the type property name for this bean.
846    *
847    * <p>
848    * This is the name of the bean property used to store the dictionary name of a bean type so that the parser knows
849    * the data type to reconstruct.
850    *
851    * <p>
852    * If <jk>null</jk>, <js>"_type"</js> should be assumed.
853    *
854    * <p>
855    * The value is determined from:
856    * <ul>
857    *    <li>The marshalling-side {@code @Marshalled(typePropertyName)} on the class, if present
858    *       (resolved via {@link BeanMetaInitializer#resolveTypePropertyName(BeanConfigContext, ClassInfo)}).
859    *    <li>Otherwise, the default from {@link BeanConfigContext#getBeanTypePropertyName()}.
860    * </ul>
861    *
862    * @return
863    *    The type property name associated with this bean, or <jk>null</jk> if the default <js>"_type"</js> should be used.
864    * @see BeanConfigContext#getBeanTypePropertyName()
865    */
866   public String getTypePropertyName() { return typePropertyName; }
867
868   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
869   public int hashCode() {
870      return classInfo.hashCode();
871   }
872
873   /**
874    * Property read interceptor.
875    *
876    * <p>
877    * Called immediately after calling the getter to allow the value to be overridden.
878    *
879    * @param bean The bean from which the property was read.
880    * @param name The property name.
881    * @param value The value just extracted from calling the bean getter.
882    * @return The value to serialize.  Default is just to return the existing value.
883    */
884   public Object onReadProperty(Object bean, String name, Object value) {
885      return beanFilter == null ? value : beanFilter.readProperty(bean, name, value);
886   }
887
888   /**
889    * Property write interceptor.
890    *
891    * <p>
892    * Called immediately before calling theh setter to allow value to be overwridden.
893    *
894    * @param bean The bean from which the property was read.
895    * @param name The property name.
896    * @param value The value just parsed.
897    * @return The value to serialize.  Default is just to return the existing value.
898    */
899   public Object onWriteProperty(Object bean, String name, Object value) {
900      return beanFilter == null ? value : beanFilter.writeProperty(bean, name, value);
901   }
902
903   protected FluentMap<String,Object> properties() {
904      // @formatter:off
905      return filteredBeanPropertyMap()
906         .a(PROP_class, classInfo.getName())
907         .a(PROP_properties, properties);
908      // @formatter:on
909   }
910
911   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
912   public String toString() {
913      return r(properties());
914   }
915
916   /**
917    * Returns the bean-modeling {@link BeanTypeResolver} that this metadata was constructed against.
918    *
919    * <p>
920    * On the marshalling-side construction path, the underlying object is the marshalling context (which
921    * implements {@link BeanTypeResolver}); marshalling-side callers that need the concrete narrowing must cast
922    * (e.g. {@code (MarshallingContext) bm.getBeanTypeResolver()}).
923    *
924    * <p>
925    * Returns <jk>null</jk> when this {@link BeanMeta} was constructed via the commons-side path
926    * ({@link #of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}).  Use {@link #getConfig()} for the always-non-null bean-modeling
927    * configuration facade.
928    *
929    * @return The bean-modeling type resolver, or <jk>null</jk> for bean-modeling-only construction.
930    */
931   protected BeanTypeResolver getBeanTypeResolver() { return (BeanTypeResolver) marshallingContext; }
932
933   /**
934    * Returns the constructor for this bean, if one was found.
935    *
936    * <p>
937    * The constructor is determined by {@link #findBeanConstructor()} and may be:
938    * <ul>
939    *    <li>A constructor annotated with {@link BeanCtor @BeanCtor}
940    *    <li>An implementation class constructor (if provided)
941    *    <li>A no-argument constructor
942    *    <li><jk>null</jk> if no suitable constructor was found
943    * </ul>
944    *
945    * @return The constructor for this bean, or <jk>null</jk> if no constructor is available.
946    * @see #getConstructorArgs()
947    * @see #hasConstructor()
948    */
949   public ConstructorInfo getConstructor() {
950      return beanConstructor.constructor().orElse(null);
951   }
952
953   /**
954    * Returns the list of property names that correspond to the constructor parameters.
955    *
956    * <p>
957    * The property names are in the same order as the constructor parameters. These names are used to map
958    * parsed property values to constructor arguments when creating bean instances.
959    *
960    * <p>
961    * The property names are determined from:
962    * <ul>
963    *    <li>The {@link BeanCtor#properties() properties()} value in the {@link BeanCtor @BeanCtor} annotation (if present)
964    *    <li>Otherwise, the parameter names from the constructor (if available in bytecode)
965    * </ul>
966    *
967    * <p>
968    * If the bean has no constructor or uses a no-argument constructor, this list will be empty.
969    *
970    * @return A list of property names corresponding to constructor parameters, in parameter order.
971    * @see #getConstructor()
972    * @see #hasConstructor()
973    */
974   public List<String> getConstructorArgs() {
975      return beanConstructor.args();
976   }
977
978   /**
979    * Returns the "extras" property for dynamic bean properties.
980    *
981    * @return The dynamic property, or <jk>null</jk> if not present.
982    */
983   protected BeanPropertyMeta getDynaProperty() { return dynaProperty; }
984
985   /**
986    * Returns the map of getter methods to property names.
987    *
988    * @return The getter properties map.
989    */
990   protected Map<Method,String> getGetterProps() { return getterProps; }
991
992   /**
993    * Returns the map of hidden properties on this bean.
994    *
995    * <p>
996    * Hidden properties are properties that exist on the bean but are not included in the normal property list.
997    * These properties are typically excluded from serialization but may still be accessible programmatically.
998    *
999    * <p>
1000    * Hidden properties can be defined through:
1001    * <ul>
1002    *    <li>{@link BeanFilter#getExcludeProperties() Bean filter exclude properties}
1003    *    <li>Properties that fail validation during bean metadata creation
1004    * </ul>
1005    *
1006    * @return A map of hidden property names to their metadata. The map is unmodifiable.
1007    * @see #getProperties()
1008    */
1009   protected Map<String,BeanPropertyMeta> getHiddenProperties() {
1010      return hiddenProperties;
1011   }
1012
1013   /**
1014    * Returns the map of setter methods to property names.
1015    *
1016    * @return The setter properties map.
1017    */
1018   protected Map<Method,String> getSetterProps() { return setterProps; }
1019
1020   /**
1021    * Returns whether this bean has a constructor available for instantiation.
1022    *
1023    * <p>
1024    * A bean has a constructor if {@link #findBeanConstructor()} was able to find a suitable constructor,
1025    * which may be:
1026    * <ul>
1027    *    <li>A constructor annotated with {@link BeanCtor @BeanCtor}
1028    *    <li>An implementation class constructor (if provided)
1029    *    <li>A no-argument constructor
1030    * </ul>
1031    *
1032    * <p>
1033    * If this method returns <jk>false</jk>, the bean cannot be instantiated using {@link #newBean(Object)},
1034    * and may need to be created through other means (e.g., interface proxies).
1035    *
1036    * @return <jk>true</jk> if a constructor is available, <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
1037    * @see #getConstructor()
1038    * @see #newBean(Object)
1039    */
1040   public boolean hasConstructor() {
1041      return beanConstructor.constructor().isPresent();
1042   }
1043
1044   /**
1045    * Returns whether this bean has opted out of alphabetical property sorting.
1046    *
1047    * @return <jk>true</jk> if properties should use natural JVM-dependent order.
1048    */
1049   protected boolean isUnsortedProperties() { return unsortedProperties; }
1050
1051   /**
1052    * Creates a new instance of this bean.
1053    *
1054    * @param outer The outer object if bean class is a non-static inner member class.
1055    * @return A new instance of this bean if possible, or <jk>null</jk> if not.
1056    * @throws ExecutableException Exception occurred on invoked constructor/method/field.
1057    */
1058   @SuppressWarnings({
1059      "unchecked", // Type erasure requires unchecked cast
1060      "rawtypes" // Raw BeanFactory type used at runtime for factory resolution
1061   })
1062   public T newBean(Object outer) throws ExecutableException {
1063      if (factoryClass != null) {
1064         try {
1065            BeanFactory factory = resolveFactory(factoryClass);
1066            return (T) factory.create();
1067         } catch (ExecutableException e) {
1068            throw e;
1069         } catch (Exception e) {
1070            throw new ExecutableException(e);
1071         }
1072      }
1073      if (classInfo.isMemberClass() && classInfo.isNotStatic()) {
1074         if (hasConstructor())
1075            return getConstructor().<T>newInstance(outer);
1076      } else {
1077         if (hasConstructor())
1078            return getConstructor().<T>newInstance();
1079         var h = beanProxyInvocationHandler.get();
1080         if (nn(h)) {
1081            var inner = classInfo.inner();
1082            return (T)Proxy.newProxyInstance(inner.getClassLoader(), a(inner, Serializable.class), h);
1083         }
1084      }
1085      return null;
1086   }
1087
1088   @SuppressWarnings({
1089      "rawtypes", // Raw BeanFactory type at runtime
1090      "unchecked" // Unchecked casts required for factory class and BeanStore result
1091   })
1092   private BeanFactory resolveFactory(Class<? extends BeanFactory> fc) {
1093      var bs = config.getBeanStore();
1094      if (bs != null) {
1095         var opt = bs.getBean(fc);
1096         if (opt.isPresent())
1097            return opt.get();
1098      }
1099      return (BeanFactory) BeanInstantiator.of((Class)fc).run();
1100   }
1101
1102   /*
1103    * Finds the appropriate constructor for this bean and determines the property names for constructor arguments.
1104    *
1105    * <p>
1106    * This method searches for a constructor in the following order of precedence:
1107    * <ol>
1108    *    <li><b>{@link BeanCtor @BeanCtor} annotated constructor:</b> If a constructor is annotated with {@link BeanCtor @BeanCtor},
1109    *       it is used. The property names are determined from:
1110    *       <ul>
1111    *          <li>The {@link BeanCtor#properties() properties()} value in the annotation, if specified
1112    *          <li>Otherwise, the parameter names from the constructor (if available in bytecode)
1113    *       </ul>
1114    *       If multiple constructors are annotated with {@link BeanCtor @BeanCtor}, an exception is thrown.
1115    *    <li><b>Implementation class constructor:</b> If an {@link #implClassConstructor} was provided during bean
1116    *       metadata creation, it is used with an empty property list.
1117    *    <li><b>No-arg constructor:</b> Searches for a no-argument constructor. The visibility required depends on
1118    *       whether the class carries a bean-registration annotation (e.g. {@link BeanType @BeanType} or the
1119    *       marshalling-side {@code @Marshalled}) — see
1120    *       {@link BeanMetaInitializer#hasBeanRegistrationAnnotation(BeanConfigContext, ClassInfo)}:
1121    *       <ul>
1122    *          <li>If a registration annotation is present, private constructors are allowed
1123    *          <li>Otherwise, the visibility is determined by {@link BeanConfigContext#getBeanConstructorVisibility()}
1124    *       </ul>
1125    *    <li><b>No constructor:</b> Returns an empty {@link Optional} if no suitable constructor is found.
1126    * </ol>
1127    *
1128    * <p>
1129    * The returned {@link BeanConstructor} contains:
1130    * <ul>
1131    *    <li>The constructor (if found), wrapped in an {@link Optional}
1132    *    <li>A list of property names that correspond to the constructor parameters, in order
1133    * </ul>
1134    *
1135    * @return A {@link BeanConstructor} containing the found constructor and its associated property names.
1136    * @throws BeanRuntimeException If multiple constructors are annotated with {@link BeanCtor @BeanCtor}, or if
1137    *    the number of properties specified in {@link BeanCtor @BeanCtor} doesn't match the number of constructor parameters,
1138    *    or if parameter names cannot be determined from the bytecode.
1139    */
1140   @SuppressWarnings({
1141      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for constructor finding logic
1142   })
1143   private BeanConstructor findBeanConstructor() {
1144      var ap = config.getAnnotationProvider();
1145      var vis = config.getBeanConstructorVisibility();
1146      var ci = classInfo;
1147
1148      var l = ci.getPublicConstructors().stream().filter(x -> ap.has(BeanCtor.class, x)).toList();
1149      if (l.isEmpty())
1150         l = ci.getDeclaredConstructors().stream().filter(x -> ap.has(BeanCtor.class, x)).toList();
1151      if (l.size() > 1)
1152         throw brex(ci, "Multiple instances of '@BeanCtor' found.");
1153      if (l.size() == 1) {
1154         var con = first(l).orElseThrow(() -> brex(ci, "No constructor found.")).accessible();
1155         var args = ap.find(BeanCtor.class, con).stream().map(x -> x.inner().properties()).filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank).map(x -> split(x)).findFirst().orElse(emptyList());
1156         if (! con.hasNumParameters(args.size())) {
1157            if (ine(args))
1158               throw brex(ci, "Number of properties defined in '@BeanCtor' annotation does not match number of parameters in constructor.");
1159            args = con.getParameters().stream().map(x -> x.getName()).toList();
1160            for (int i = 0; i < args.size(); i++) {
1161               if (isBlank(args.get(i)))
1162                  throw brex(ci, "Could not find name for parameter #{0} of constructor ''{1}''", i, con.getNameFull());
1163            }
1164         }
1165         return new BeanConstructor(o(con), args);
1166      }
1167
1168      if (ci.isRecord()) {
1169         var components = ci.getRecordComponents();
1170         var paramTypes = components.stream().map(RecordComponent::getType).toArray(Class[]::new);
1171         var rcon = ci.getPublicConstructor(x -> x.hasParameterTypes(paramTypes)).orElse(null);
1172         if (rcon != null)
1173            return new BeanConstructor(o(rcon.accessible()), components.stream().map(RecordComponent::getName).toList());
1174      }
1175
1176      if (implClassConstructor != null)
1177         return new BeanConstructor(o(implClassConstructor.accessible()), emptyList());
1178
1179      var con = ci.getNoArgConstructor(config.getBeanMetaInitializer().hasBeanRegistrationAnnotation(config, classInfo) ? Visibility.PRIVATE : vis).orElse(null);
1180      if (con != null)
1181         return new BeanConstructor(o(con.accessible()), emptyList());
1182
1183      return new BeanConstructor(oe(), emptyList());
1184   }
1185
1186   /**
1187    * Resolves the bean property name for a field when {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} or {@link Name @Name} supplies
1188    * <js>"*"</js>.
1189    *
1190    * <p>
1191    * On a {@link Map} field, <js>"*"</js> remains the dyna-property name. On any other field type, <js>"*"</js> is
1192    * treated like an unnamed {@code @BeanProp} (apply other attributes) but the property name is taken from the field
1193    * (via {@link PropertyNamer#getPropertyName(String)}).
1194    * </p>
1195    *
1196    * @param x Field being registered.
1197    * @param nameFromAnnotations Name from {@code @BeanProp}/{@code @Name}, or already-resolved default from the field name.
1198    * @param propertyNamer Namer for raw field names.
1199    * @return Property key to use in {@link BeanMeta}.
1200    */
1201   private static String resolveBeanFieldPropertyName(FieldInfo x, String nameFromAnnotations, PropertyNamer propertyNamer) {
1202      if (! "*".equals(nameFromAnnotations))
1203         return nameFromAnnotations;
1204      if (x.getFieldType().isAssignableTo(Map.class))
1205         return "*";
1206      return propertyNamer.getPropertyName(x.getName());
1207   }
1208
1209   /*
1210    * Finds all bean fields in the class hierarchy.
1211    *
1212    * <p>
1213    * Traverses the complete class hierarchy (as defined by {@link #classHierarchy}) and collects all fields that
1214    * meet the bean field criteria:
1215    * <ul>
1216    *    <li>Not static
1217    *    <li>Not transient (unless transient fields are not ignored)
1218    *    <li>Not annotated with {@link Transient @Transient} (unless transient fields are not ignored)
1219    *    <li>Not annotated with {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore}
1220    *    <li>Visible according to the specified visibility level, or annotated with {@link BeanProp @BeanProp}
1221    * </ul>
1222    *
1223    * @return A collection of all bean fields found in the class hierarchy.
1224    */
1225   private Collection<FieldInfo> findBeanFields() {
1226      var v = config.getBeanFieldVisibility();
1227      var noIgnoreTransients = ! config.isIgnoreTransientFields();
1228      var ap = config.getAnnotationProvider();
1229      var isRecord = classInfo.isRecord();
1230      var recordComponentNames = isRecord
1231         ? classInfo.getRecordComponents().stream().map(RecordComponent::getName).collect(java.util.stream.Collectors.toSet())
1232         : Set.<String>of();
1233      // @formatter:off
1234      return classHierarchy.get().stream()
1235         .flatMap(c2 -> c2.getDeclaredFields().stream())
1236         .filter(x -> x.isNotStatic()
1237            && (x.isNotTransient() || noIgnoreTransients)
1238            && (! x.hasAnnotation(Transient.class) || noIgnoreTransients)
1239            && ! ap.has(BeanIgnore.class, x)
1240            && (v.isVisible(x.inner()) || ap.has(BeanProp.class, x)
1241               || (isRecord && recordComponentNames.contains(x.getName()))))
1242         .toList();
1243      // @formatter:on
1244   }
1245
1246   /*
1247    * Finds all bean methods (getters, setters, and extraKeys) in the class hierarchy.
1248    *
1249    * <p>
1250    * Traverses the complete class hierarchy (as defined by {@link #classHierarchy}) and identifies methods that
1251    * represent bean properties. Methods are identified as:
1252    * <ul>
1253    *    <li><b>Getters:</b> Methods with no parameters that return a value, matching patterns like:
1254    *       <ul>
1255    *          <li><c>getX()</c> - standard getter pattern
1256    *          <li><c>isX()</c> - boolean getter pattern (returns boolean or Boolean)
1257    *          <li>Methods annotated with {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} or {@link Name @Name}
1258    *          <li>Methods with {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotation with value <js>"*"</js> that return a Map
1259    *       </ul>
1260    *    <li><b>Setters:</b> Methods with one parameter that match patterns like:
1261    *       <ul>
1262    *          <li><c>setX(value)</c> - standard setter pattern
1263    *          <li><c>withX(value)</c> - fluent setter pattern (returns the bean type)
1264    *          <li>Methods annotated with {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} or {@link Name @Name}
1265    *          <li>Methods with {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotation with value <js>"*"</js> that accept a Map
1266    *          <li>Fluent setters (if enabled) - methods that return the bean type and accept one parameter
1267    *       </ul>
1268    *    <li><b>ExtraKeys:</b> Methods with {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotation with value <js>"*"</js> that return a Collection
1269    * </ul>
1270    *
1271    * <p>
1272    * Methods are filtered based on:
1273    * <ul>
1274    *    <li>Not static, not bridge methods
1275    *    <li>Parameter count ≤ 2
1276    *    <li>Not annotated with {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore}
1277    *    <li>Not annotated with {@link Transient @Transient}
1278    *    <li>Visible according to the specified visibility level, or annotated with {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} or {@link Name @Name}
1279    * </ul>
1280    *
1281    * <p>
1282    * Property names are determined from:
1283    * <ul>
1284    *    <li>{@link BeanProp @BeanProp} or {@link Name @Name} annotations (if present)
1285    *    <li>Otherwise, derived from the method name using the provided {@link PropertyNamer}
1286    * </ul>
1287    *
1288    * @return A list of {@link BeanMethod} objects representing all found bean methods.
1289    */
1290   @SuppressWarnings({
1291      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for bean method finding logic
1292   })
1293   private List<BeanMethod> findBeanMethods() {
1294      var l = new LinkedList<BeanMethod>();
1295      var ap = config.getAnnotationProvider();
1296      var ci = classInfo;
1297      var v = config.getBeanMethodVisibility();
1298      var pn = o(beanFilter).map(x -> x.getPropertyNamer()).orElse(config.getPropertyNamer());
1299      var suppressedFromBeanIgnoredFields = findSuppressedPropertyNamesFromIgnoredFields(pn);
1300
1301      classHierarchy.get().forEach(c2 -> {
1302         for (var m : c2.getDeclaredMethods()) {
1303            var mm = m.getMatchingMethods();
1304            var beanps = ap.find(BeanProp.class, m).stream().map(AnnotationInfo::inner).toList();
1305            var names = ap.find(Name.class, m).stream().map(AnnotationInfo::inner).toList();
1306            // Skip static, bridge, or methods with >2 params; skip if ignored, transient, or not visible
1307            if (m.isStatic() || m.isBridge() || m.getParameterCount() > 2
1308               || mm.stream().anyMatch(m2 -> ap.has(BeanIgnore.class, m2, SELF))
1309               || mm.stream().anyMatch(m2 -> ap.find(Transient.class, m2, SELF).stream().map(x -> x.inner().value()).findFirst().orElse(false))
1310               || ! (m.isVisible(v) || ine(beanps) || ine(names)))
1311               continue;
1312
1313            var n = m.getNameSimple();
1314
1315            var params = m.getParameters();
1316            var rt = m.getReturnType();
1317            var methodType = UNKNOWN;
1318            var bpName = bpName(beanps, names);
1319
1320            if (params.isEmpty()) {
1321               if ("*".equals(bpName)) {
1322                  if (rt.isAssignableTo(Collection.class)) {
1323                     methodType = EXTRAKEYS;
1324                  } else if (rt.isAssignableTo(Map.class)) {
1325                     methodType = GETTER;
1326                  }
1327                  n = bpName;
1328               } else if (n.startsWith("get") && (! rt.is(Void.TYPE))) {
1329                  methodType = GETTER;
1330                  n = n.substring(3);
1331               } else if (n.startsWith("is") && (rt.is(Boolean.TYPE) || rt.is(Boolean.class))) {
1332                  methodType = GETTER;
1333                  n = n.substring(2);
1334               } else if (ci.isRecord() && isRecordAccessor(m, ci)) {
1335                  methodType = GETTER;
1336               } else if (nn(bpName)) {
1337                  methodType = GETTER;
1338                  if (bpName.isEmpty()) {
1339                     if (n.startsWith("get"))
1340                        n = n.substring(3);
1341                     else if (n.startsWith("is"))
1342                        n = n.substring(2);
1343                     bpName = n;
1344                  } else {
1345                     n = bpName;
1346                  }
1347               }
1348            } else if (params.size() == 1) {
1349               if ("*".equals(bpName)) {
1350                  if (params.get(0).getParameterType().isAssignableTo(Map.class)) {
1351                     methodType = SETTER;
1352                     n = bpName;
1353                  } else if (params.get(0).getParameterType().is(String.class)) {
1354                     methodType = GETTER;
1355                     n = bpName;
1356                  }
1357               } else if (n.startsWith("set") && (rt.isAssignableFrom(ci) || rt.is(Void.TYPE))) {
1358                  methodType = SETTER;
1359                  n = n.substring(3);
1360               } else if (n.startsWith("with") && (rt.isAssignableFrom(ci))) {
1361                  methodType = SETTER;
1362                  n = n.substring(4);
1363               } else if (nn(bpName)) {
1364                  methodType = SETTER;
1365                  if (bpName.isEmpty()) {
1366                     if (n.startsWith("set"))
1367                        n = n.substring(3);
1368                     bpName = n;
1369                  } else {
1370                     n = bpName;
1371                  }
1372               } else if (fluentSetters && rt.isAssignableFrom(ci)) {
1373                  methodType = SETTER;
1374               }
1375            } else if (params.size() == 2) {
1376               if ("*".equals(bpName) && params.get(0).getParameterType().is(String.class) && n.startsWith("set") && (rt.isAssignableFrom(ci) || rt.is(Void.TYPE))) {
1377                  methodType = SETTER;
1378               } else {
1379                  methodType = GETTER;
1380               }
1381               n = bpName;
1382            }
1383            n = pn.getPropertyName(n);
1384
1385            if ("*".equals(bpName) && methodType == UNKNOWN)
1386               throw brex(ci, "Found @BeanProp(\"*\") but could not determine method type on method ''{0}''.", m.getNameSimple());
1387
1388            if (methodType != UNKNOWN) {
1389               if (nn(bpName) && ! bpName.isEmpty())
1390                  n = bpName;
1391               if (nn(n) && ! suppressedFromBeanIgnoredFields.contains(n))
1392                  l.add(new BeanMethod(n, methodType, m.inner()));
1393            }
1394         }
1395      });
1396      return l;
1397   }
1398
1399   private static boolean isRecordAccessor(MethodInfo m, ClassInfo ci) {
1400      return ci.getRecordComponents().stream()
1401         .anyMatch(rc -> rc.getName().equals(m.getNameSimple())
1402            && rc.getType().equals(m.getReturnType().inner()));
1403   }
1404
1405   /*
1406    * Creates a bean registry for this bean class.
1407    *
1408    * <p>
1409    * The bean registry is used to resolve dictionary names (type names) to actual class types. This is essential
1410    * for polymorphic bean serialization and parsing, where a dictionary name in the serialized form needs to be
1411    * mapped back to the correct subclass.
1412    *
1413    * <p>
1414    * The registry is built from:
1415    * <ul>
1416    *    <li><b>Bean filter dictionary:</b> Classes specified in the {@link BeanFilter#getBeanDictionary() bean filter's dictionary}
1417    *       (if a bean filter is present)
1418    *    <li><b>{@code @Marshalled} annotation:</b> If the class has a {@code @Marshalled} annotation with a non-empty
1419    *       {@code typeName()}, the class itself is added to the dictionary
1420    * </ul>
1421    *
1422    * <p>
1423    * The registry is used by parsers to determine which class to instantiate when deserializing polymorphic beans.
1424    *
1425    * @return A new registry lookup containing the dictionary classes for this bean, or an empty lookup if
1426    *    no dictionary classes are found.
1427    */
1428   private BeanRegistryLookup findBeanRegistry() {
1429      // BeanRegistry is a marshalling-side concern (polymorphic dispatch via @Marshalled(typeName)/dictionary).
1430      // Lifted out to the BeanMetaInitializer SPI carried on BeanConfigContext so this class no longer references
1431      // MarshalledBeanMetaInitializer directly.  Returns null on the commons-side path (no marshallingContext).
1432      return config.getBeanMetaInitializer().buildBeanRegistry(marshallingContext, beanFilter, classInfo, config);
1433   }
1434
1435   /*
1436    * Builds a list of all classes in the class hierarchy for this bean.
1437    *
1438    * <p>
1439    * Traverses the complete inheritance hierarchy (classes and interfaces) starting from the bean class (or the
1440    * interface class specified in the bean filter) and collects all classes up to (but not including) the stop class.
1441    *
1442    * <p>
1443    * The traversal order follows a depth-first approach:
1444    * <ol>
1445    *    <li>First, recursively traverses the superclass hierarchy
1446    *    <li>Then, recursively traverses all implemented interfaces
1447    *    <li>Finally, adds the current class itself
1448    * </ol>
1449    *
1450    * <p>
1451    * If a {@link BeanFilter#getInterfaceClass() bean filter interface class} is specified, the traversal starts
1452    * from that interface class instead of the bean class itself. This allows beans to use properties defined on
1453    * a parent interface rather than the concrete implementation class.
1454    *
1455    * <p>
1456    * The resulting list is used to find bean properties, methods, and fields across the entire class hierarchy.
1457    *
1458    * @return An unmodifiable list of {@link ClassInfo} objects representing all classes in the hierarchy, in
1459    *    traversal order (superclasses first, then interfaces, then the class itself).
1460    */
1461   private List<ClassInfo> findClassHierarchy() {
1462      var result = new LinkedList<ClassInfo>();
1463      // If @BeanType(interfaceClass) is specified on the parent class, then we want
1464      // to use the properties defined on that class, not the subclass.
1465      var c2 = (nn(beanFilter) && nn(beanFilter.getInterfaceClass()) ? beanFilter.getInterfaceClass() : classInfo);
1466      findClassHierarchy(c2, stopClass, result::add);
1467      return u(result);
1468   }
1469
1470   /*
1471    * Recursively traverses the class hierarchy and invokes the consumer for each class found.
1472    *
1473    * <p>
1474    * This is a helper method that performs a depth-first traversal of the class hierarchy:
1475    * <ol>
1476    *    <li>Recursively processes the superclass (if present and not the stop class)
1477    *    <li>Recursively processes all implemented interfaces
1478    *    <li>Invokes the consumer with the current class
1479    * </ol>
1480    *
1481    * <p>
1482    * The traversal stops when it reaches the stop class (which is not included in the traversal).
1483    *
1484    * @param c The class to start traversal from.
1485    * @param stopClass The class to stop traversal at (exclusive). Traversal will not proceed beyond this class.
1486    * @param consumer The consumer to invoke for each class in the hierarchy.
1487    */
1488   private void findClassHierarchy(ClassInfo c, ClassInfo stopClass, Consumer<ClassInfo> consumer) {
1489      var sc = c.getSuperclass();
1490      if (nn(sc) && ! sc.is(stopClass.inner()))
1491         findClassHierarchy(sc, stopClass, consumer);
1492      c.getInterfaces().forEach(x -> findClassHierarchy(x, stopClass, consumer));
1493      consumer.accept(c);
1494   }
1495
1496   /*
1497    * Finds the dictionary name (type name) for this bean class.
1498    *
1499    * <p>
1500    * The dictionary name is used in serialized forms to identify the specific type of a bean instance, enabling
1501    * polymorphic serialization and deserialization. This is especially important when serializing/parsing beans
1502    * that are part of an inheritance hierarchy.
1503    *
1504    * <p>
1505    * The dictionary name is determined by searching in the following order of precedence:
1506    * <ol>
1507    *    <li><b>Bean filter type name:</b> If a bean filter is present and has a type name specified via
1508    *       {@link BeanFilter#getTypeName()}, that value is used.
1509    *    <li><b>Bean registry lookup:</b> If a bean registry exists for this bean, it is queried for the type name
1510    *       of this class.
1511    *    <li><b>Parent class registry lookup:</b> Searches through parent classes and interfaces (starting from the
1512    *       second one, skipping the class itself) and checks if any of their bean registries contain a type name
1513    *       for this class.
1514    *    <li><b>{@code @Marshalled} annotation:</b> If the class has a {@code @Marshalled} annotation with a non-empty
1515    *       {@code typeName()}, that value is used.
1516    *    <li><b>No dictionary name:</b> Returns <jk>null</jk> if no dictionary name is found.
1517    * </ol>
1518    *
1519    * <p>
1520    * If a dictionary name is found, it will be used in serialized output (typically as a special property like
1521    * <js>"_type"</js>) so that parsers can determine the correct class to instantiate when deserializing.
1522    *
1523    * @return The dictionary name for this bean, or <jk>null</jk> if no dictionary name is defined.
1524    */
1525   private String findDictionaryName() {
1526      if (nn(beanFilter) && nn(beanFilter.getTypeName()))
1527         return beanFilter.getTypeName();
1528
1529      // Pure-reflection class identity for BeanRegistry.getTypeName(...) — this method now uses
1530      // surviving `this.classMeta` references inside this method to `classInfo.inner()` so the dictionary
1531      // lookup does not require a ClassMeta for the bean's own class.  BeanRegistry still lives in
1532      // juneau-marshall; it just exposes a raw-Class overload for callers that have a ClassInfo.
1533      var rawClass = classInfo.inner();
1534
1535      var br = getBeanRegistry();
1536      if (nn(br)) {
1537         String s = br.getTypeName(rawClass);
1538         if (nn(s))
1539            return s;
1540      }
1541
1542      // Parent-class BeanRegistry lookup is a marshalling-side concern: it walks parents and interfaces to
1543      // see if any of THEIR ClassMeta-backed BeanRegistries declares a typeName for our raw class.  Lifted
1544      // out to the BeanMetaInitializer SPI carried on BeanConfigContext.  Returns null on the commons-side path.
1545      var bmi = config.getBeanMetaInitializer();
1546      var n = bmi.findTypeNameInParents(marshallingContext, classInfo, rawClass);
1547      if (n != null)
1548         return n;
1549
1550      return bmi.findMarshalledTypeName(config, classInfo);
1551   }
1552
1553   /*
1554    * Merges standard JavaBeans {@link BeanInfo} property descriptors into {@code normalProps}, skipping the class
1555    * pseudo-property and logical names suppressed when {@link BeanIgnore#ignoreAccessors()} is <jk>true</jk> on a field.
1556    */
1557   @SuppressWarnings({
1558      "java:S135" // Two continues: skip class pseudo-property and names suppressed via @BeanIgnore(ignoreAccessors)
1559   })
1560   private void mergeJavaBeanPropertyDescriptorsIntoNormalProps(java.beans.BeanInfo bi, Map<String,BeanPropertyMeta.Builder> normalProps,
1561         PropertyNamer propertyNamer) {
1562      var suppressedFromBeanIgnoredFields = findSuppressedPropertyNamesFromIgnoredFields(propertyNamer);
1563      for (var pd : bi.getPropertyDescriptors()) {
1564         if (PROP_class.equals(pd.getName()))
1565            continue;
1566         if (suppressedFromBeanIgnoredFields.contains(pd.getName()))
1567            continue;
1568         var builder = normalProps.computeIfAbsent(pd.getName(), n -> BeanPropertyMeta.builder(this, n));
1569         if (pd.getReadMethod() != null)
1570            builder.setGetter(info(pd.getReadMethod()));
1571         if (pd.getWriteMethod() != null)
1572            builder.setSetter(info(pd.getWriteMethod()));
1573      }
1574   }
1575
1576   /*
1577    * Property names suppressed from getter/setter discovery because a non-static field with that logical name is
1578    * annotated with {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore} and {@link BeanIgnore#ignoreAccessors()} is <jk>true</jk>.
1579    *
1580    * <p>
1581    * When {@link BeanIgnore#ignoreAccessors()} is <jk>false</jk> (the default), ignored fields do not suppress
1582    * JavaBean accessors so patterns such as {@code @BeanIgnore} on a private field with a public {@code getX()} still
1583    * expose {@code x} when field visibility excludes the field.
1584    */
1585   @SuppressWarnings({
1586      "java:S135" // Two continues in inner loop: skip fields without @BeanIgnore or without ignoreAccessors
1587   })
1588   private Set<String> findSuppressedPropertyNamesFromIgnoredFields(PropertyNamer propertyNamer) {
1589      var s = new HashSet<String>();
1590      var ap = config.getAnnotationProvider();
1591      for (var c2 : classHierarchy.get()) {
1592         for (var x : c2.getDeclaredFields()) {
1593            if (! x.isNotStatic() || ! ap.has(BeanIgnore.class, x))
1594               continue;
1595            if (! fieldBeanIgnoreIgnoresAccessors(x))
1596               continue;
1597            var name = ap.find(x).stream()
1598               .filter(x2 -> x2.isType(BeanProp.class) || x2.isType(Name.class))
1599               .map(BeanMeta::name)
1600               .filter(Objects::nonNull)
1601               .findFirst()
1602               .orElse(propertyNamer.getPropertyName(x.getName()));
1603            name = resolveBeanFieldPropertyName(x, name, propertyNamer);
1604            if (nn(name))
1605               s.add(name);
1606         }
1607      }
1608      return s;
1609   }
1610
1611   private static boolean fieldBeanIgnoreIgnoresAccessors(FieldInfo x) {
1612      for (var bi : x.inner().getAnnotationsByType(BeanIgnore.class))
1613         if (bi.ignoreAccessors())
1614            return true;
1615      return false;
1616   }
1617
1618   /*
1619    * Finds a bean field by name in the class hierarchy.
1620    *
1621    * <p>
1622    * Searches through the complete class hierarchy (as defined by {@link #classHierarchy}) to find a field
1623    * with the specified name. The search is performed in the order classes appear in the hierarchy, and the
1624    * first matching field is returned.
1625    *
1626    * <p>
1627    * A field is considered a match if it:
1628    * <ul>
1629    *    <li>Has the exact name specified
1630    *    <li>Is not static
1631    *    <li>Is not transient (unless transient fields are not ignored)
1632    *    <li>Is not annotated with {@link Transient @Transient} (unless transient fields are not ignored)
1633    *    <li>Is not annotated with {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore}
1634    * </ul>
1635    *
1636    * <p>
1637    * This method is used to find fields for bean properties when a field reference is needed but wasn't
1638    * discovered during the initial property discovery phase (e.g., for properties defined only through
1639    * getters/setters).
1640    *
1641    * @param name The name of the field to find.
1642    * @return The {@link FieldInfo} for the field if found, or <jk>null</jk> if no matching field exists
1643    *    in the class hierarchy.
1644    */
1645   private Optional<FieldInfo> findInnerBeanField(String name) {
1646      var noIgnoreTransients = ! config.isIgnoreTransientFields();
1647      var ap = config.getAnnotationProvider();
1648
1649      // @formatter:off
1650      return classHierarchy.get().stream()
1651         .flatMap(c2 -> c2.getDeclaredField(
1652            x -> x.isNotStatic()
1653               && (x.isNotTransient() || noIgnoreTransients)
1654               && (! x.hasAnnotation(Transient.class) || noIgnoreTransients)
1655               && ! ap.has(BeanIgnore.class, x)
1656               && x.hasName(name)
1657         ).stream())
1658         .findFirst();
1659      // @formatter:on
1660   }
1661}
